# wallhaven

```go
import "github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/internal/wallhaven"
```

Package wallhaven fetches wallpapers from the Wallhaven API into a local cache directory, which then acts as a regular category source. Like the weather package, it is best\-effort: a network failure never breaks a category that already has cached images.

## Index

- [func Refresh\(cfg Config, cacheDir string\) error](<#Refresh>)
- [type Config](<#Config>)


<a name="Refresh"></a>
## func [Refresh](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/wallhaven/wallhaven.go#L55>)

```go
func Refresh(cfg Config, cacheDir string) error
```

Refresh fetches one new image matching cfg into cacheDir \(creating it if needed\) and prunes cacheDir down to cfg.CacheLimit \(oldest first by modification time\). Failures leave the existing cache untouched, so a category with a populated cache keeps working offline; the returned error is for the caller to log.

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/wallhaven/wallhaven.go#L28-L33>)

Config is one refresh request's parameters.

```go
type Config struct {
    Query      string
    Purity     string // sfw (default) | sketchy | nsfw
    APIKey     string
    CacheLimit int // 0 = default (100)
}
```
